title: Improved IVF outcomes with time-lapse embryo imaging: a randomized controlled trial
content: Time-lapse embryo imaging has been proposed as a non-invasive method to improve embryo selection in IVF cycles. We conducted a multicenter randomized controlled trial involving 1,200 patients undergoing IVF treatment. The live birth rate was significantly higher in the time-lapse group compared to the control group (48.2% vs. 39.5%, relative risk 1.22, 95% CI 1.06-1.40, p=0.006). Time-lapse embryo imaging significantly improves live birth rates compared to standard morphological assessment in IVF patients.
